meaning: n.宗派,教派,教士会,流派,部分,段
examples: Every conceivable Protestant sect was publishing treatises of theological speculation and religious propaganda at a surprising rate.
Rarely do the Amish marry outside their sect.
Members of the small Yazidi sect which live in the area were the apparent targets.
Soon, people outside the sect began to seek out Quakers as trading partners, suppliers and sellers.
Hutterites are an Anabaptist sect, founded by Austrian preacher Jakob Hutter, in the 16th Century.
